You are a senior architect reviewing this plan for a POC. Do the following in a loop until you have no more concerns:

Critique — Identify every assumption, gap, risk, or unclear decision in the current plan. Be ruthless.
Research — For each concern, reason through the best available options, trade-offs, and known pitfalls.
Revise — Update the plan to address all concerns.
Verify — Re-read the revised plan and ask: "Is there anything I'm still not 100% sure about?" If yes, go back to step 1
For each major decision, describe the strongest alternative approach and why you rejected it.

Only stop when you can confidently say every decision is justified, every dependency is accounted for, and there are no open questions. Output the final plan with a section called "Decisions & Rationale" explaining why each key choice was made.

Do not finalize the plan until you have zero open questions and can justify every decision with evidence or reasoning. If you're unsure about anything, say so explicitly and resolve it before moving on.
